# Haven - MIMI Conformance Statement

- **Implementation version:** `0.0.1-pre`
- **Generated:** 2026-07-15
- **v1 scope:** add-driven, text/message-only cross-provider chat on MLS ciphersuite 0x0001

> We do **not** claim 100% MIMI conformance. This document states, per draft and per
> normative requirement, exactly what Haven supports, what it deliberately diverges on (and
> why), and what is out of scope, so the claim is verifiable, not marketing.

BUILT today (suite 0x0001, add-driven, text/message): a conformant MIMI provider for directory, keyMaterial publish/claim, Welcome relay, message store-and-forward, and opt-in identifierQuery, fronted by mTLS + a two-sided ciphersuite gate; MIMI content-09 framing is cross-impl-verified byte-identical against all 14 official IETF test vectors; and TRUE TWO-PROVIDER FEDERATION is live-proven: a real end-to-end MLS exchange runs cross-provider between two independent providers (A on Core, B on relay2): mimi:// addressing + well-known discovery drive the route, KeyPackage / Welcome / message relay over mTLS, a 2-provider MLS group forms (members on both authorities), and the recipient on the FOREIGN provider decrypts (proven 2026-06-15). ROADMAP (in progress, NOT yet claimed working): room-hub N-way participant-list fan-out and consent. Deliberately out of v1: external-commit join (security), non-0x0001 suites, cross-provider assets + OHTTP. Never claimed: 100% conformance.

| `draft-ietf-mls-extensions` | -10 | 2026-07-06 | yes |
| `draft-ietf-mls-virtual-clients` | -01 | 2026-07-06 | yes |

_Conformance is asserted against these exact revisions; newer revisions may diverge until this statement is regenerated._

## Deliberate divergences (read first)

These are conscious design decisions, each disclosed with its reason. A function that is flawed or opens a security hole is dropped on purpose. We do not chase an exhaustive feature list at the cost of security.

### DIV-1: No §5.6 GroupInfo / external-commit join (HavenProfile-only)  _(security)_

**What we say:** Cross-provider join is add-driven (an existing member adds you via §5.2). Self-join via external commit is unsupported by Haven's product build, permanently, by design (INV-MLS-001a), not by missing capability in the underlying library.

### DIV-2: Only MLS ciphersuite 0x0001 accepted  _(security)_

**What we say:** We pin MLS suite 0x0001; cross-provider objects in other suites are refused at ingest.

### DIV-3: No cross-provider assets / OHTTP gateway (§5.10) in v1  _(scope)_

**What we say:** v1 cross-provider messaging is text/message-only; asset transfer and its OHTTP privacy gateway are roadmap.

### DIV-4: identifierQuery is opt-in-only (stronger than the spec default)  _(privacy-upgrade)_

**What we say:** identifierQuery returns only opt-in MIMI-enrolled accounts; everyone else is indistinguishable from non-existent.

### DIV-5: Virtual Clients Variant A (provisioning state transfer) not implemented in v1  _(scope)_

**What we say:** New emulator clients onboard via external commit (§6.2) only; offline provisioning state transfer (§6.1 Variant A) is not built.

## Per-requirement conformance

### Transport & directory (protocol §4.1, §5.1)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| T1 | 4.1 | All inter-provider comms over HTTPS | both | ✅ Supported | All provider traffic runs over HTTPS/TLS, proven live between two independent providers. |
| T2 | 4.1 | Target provider in Host header | both | ✅ Supported | The client sets Host to the destination provider's authority on every request; proven live across providers. |
| T3 | 4.1 | Source provider in From header; local-part = literal 'mimi' | both | ✅ Supported | The client sets From to mimi@<source authority> on every request, per the spec's literal-local-part rule; proven live across providers. |
| T4 | 4.1 | Underlying TLS mutually authenticated (RFC 6125) | both | ✅ Supported | mTLS is enforced and verified behaviorally on both providers; a connection without a client certificate is rejected. |
| T5 | 5.1 | Publish /.well-known/mimi-protocol-directory | both | ✅ Supported | Both providers serve the directory over mTLS; a live demo client discovers a peer's directory and routes the exchange off the advertised endpoints, no hardcoded endpoints. |

### Key material (protocol §5.2)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| K1 | 5.2 | Each KeyPackage returned at most once (one-time use) | follower | ✅ Supported | KeyPackage claim is a single atomic delete-and-return; proven safe under 8-way concurrent contention with zero double-serves. |
| K2 | 5.2 | Never serve expired keying material | follower | ✅ Supported | Claim filters out any KeyPackage past its expiry in the same atomic statement; an expired KeyPackage is never served. |
| K3 | 5.2 | Hub associates KeyPackageRef -> provider | hub | ✅ Supported | The hub computes the canonical KeyPackageRef and records which provider claimed it, purely as routing metadata, so a later Welcome can be forwarded correctly. |
| K4 | 5.2 | Follower associates KeyPackageRef -> local client | follower | ✅ Supported | Publishing a KeyPackage also records which local user it belongs to, so an inbound Welcome referencing it resolves to the right recipient. |
| K5 | INV-MLS-002 | Reject inbound KeyPackage/Welcome with ciphersuite != 0x0001 before openmls | follower | ✅ Supported | Any inbound KeyPackage or Welcome in a ciphersuite other than the pinned suite is rejected before it reaches the MLS library. |

### Room state / participant list (protocol §5.3)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| R1 | 5.3+7.5 | Participant-list changes via AppSync (mimiParticipantList) | client | ✅ Supported | Participant-list changes are carried in the spec's normative §7.5 TLS presentation language via an MLS CustomProposal, proven against a real MLS library and live cross-provider. The proposal's registry codepoint is still Haven-chosen pending IANA registration. |
| R2 | 5.3+7.5 | Active-user removal simultaneous with the MLS Remove | client | ✅ Supported | A participant removal and the underlying MLS Remove are committed together in a single commit, so the two can never desync; proven live cross-provider. |
| R3 | 5.3 | Hub blocks removed participant's messages/commits/proposals (except Remove/SelfRemove) | hub | ✅ Supported | A hub relaying opaque ciphertext cannot see MLS message types inside it, so it enforces this at participant-list granularity: once removed, a member's traffic is rejected outright, stricter than the spec's own carve-out for a removed member's own Remove/SelfRemove. |

### Message submission & fanout (protocol §5.4, §5.5)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| M1 | 5.4 | App message = MLS PrivateMessage, content_type application | client | ✅ Supported | Every application message is an MLS PrivateMessage; proven live cross-provider (submit, fetch, decrypt). |
| M2 | 5.4 | sendingUri is a valid URI of an active participant | both | ✅ Supported | The hub validates that the sending URI is a real, active participant of the room before accepting a message; enforced on both the JSON and TLS-PL wire lanes. |
| M3 | 5.5 | Hub fans out accepted message to all providers with active participants + local clients | hub | 🟦 Implemented (decision logic; durable layer pending) | The local-vs-foreign routing decision is unit-tested for the multi-provider case; the multi-provider forwarding transport itself is proven only for a single foreign provider, since a third independent provider isn't available to test against. |
| M4 | 5.5 | Welcome routed per stored KeyPackageRef | hub | ✅ Supported | Welcome messages are queued and delivered once to the recipient; proven live cross-provider. Routed by recipient identity in v1 rather than by KeyPackageRef, the spec's exact form. |
| M5 | 5.5 | Follower returns 201 to a duplicate /notify (idempotent, byte-exact) | follower | ✅ Supported | A duplicate notification is detected by exact content hash and always answered the same way as the first, regardless of arrival order. |
| M6 | 5.4/5.5 | Store-and-forward delivery of an MLS PrivateMessage to a recipient (submit + deliver-once fetch) | hub | ✅ Supported | Messages are queued durably, bounded, and expire on a TTL; the provider never decrypts them. Proven live cross-provider end-to-end (submit, fetch, decrypt). |

### GroupInfo / join (protocol §5.6)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| G1 | 5.6 | Hub verifies joining-client authorization before returning GroupInfo | hub | ⬜ Out of scope (v1) | (see DIV-1) |
| G2 | 5.6 | GroupInfoResponse encrypted + signed by hub | hub | 🔴 Diverged (by design) | (see DIV-1) |

### Consent (protocol §5.7)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| C1 | 5.7 | Accept + process requestConsent from another provider | follower | ✅ Supported | A consent request is recorded durably and always answered the same way (201) regardless of whether the entry is well-formed, so the response itself leaks no existence information. Proven live as part of a full cross-provider handshake. |
| C2 | 5.7 | Accept + process updateConsent (granted/revoked/preemptive-deny) | follower | ✅ Supported | Grant, revoke, and cancel are all supported, including a preemptive revoke with no prior request; any KeyPackage carried in a grant is still ciphersuite-gated before being honored. |
| C3 | 5.2/5.7 | KeyPackage access consent-gated; identifierQuery handling | both | ✅ Supported | KeyPackage access is default-deny: no consent on record means no access, using the draft's own gate codes. The wire lane enforces the same gate as the JSON lane; the JSON demo lane's plain lookup stays ungated by design since the add-driven demo flow has no consent handshake. (see DIV-4) |

### Asset download (protocol §5.10)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| A1 | 5.10.2 | Hub verifies downloadUrl host belongs to a peer provider (anti-open-proxy) | hub | ⬜ Out of scope (v1) | (see DIV-3) |
| A2 | 5.10.3 | Hub implements an OHTTP Gateway for asset downloads | hub | ⬜ Out of scope (v1) | (see DIV-3) |

### Content format (content-09)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| F1 | 4.1 | Per-message salt cryptographically random | client | ✅ Supported | Each frame's salt is read fresh from the OS CSPRNG; two encodes of identical plaintext produce different salts. |
| F2 | 6.1 | Deterministic CBOR (RFC 8949 §4.2.1); no indefinite-length | client | ✅ Supported | The codec produces deterministic CBOR per §4.2.1 (shortest-form integers, definite-length, sorted keys); cross-implementation verified byte-identical against all 14 official IETF example vectors. |
| F10 | 4.1 | Container is the §4.1 7-element ARRAY with integer enums + FLAT NestedPart (group-choice splice), null absents, Expiration=[relative,time] | client | ✅ Supported | The container follows the spec's exact 7-element array shape; cross-implementation verified byte-identical against all 14 official IETF vectors, covering every part variant the spec defines. |
| F3 | 6.2 | Extension map keys int or text <=255 octets; valid UTF-8 | client | ✅ Supported | Extension map keys are validated on receive: integer or short UTF-8 text only, no duplicates; enforcement doesn't break byte-identical round-tripping of the official vectors. |
| F4 | 6.3 | NestedParts not nested > 4 deep | client | ✅ Supported | Nesting depth is capped at 4 and enforced on receive; a 5-deep tree is rejected by the decoder. |
| F5 | 7.1 | Receive application/mimi-content, text/plain, text/markdown;variant=GFM-MIMI | client | ✅ Supported | All three mandatory receive content-types are supported (mimi-content, plain text, GFM-MIMI markdown); unknown types fail closed. The nohtml rule is enforced everywhere a GFM-MIMI part can appear, including nested inside another content type. |
| F6 | 4.5 | Decrypt external content with AES-128-GCM | client | ⬜ Out of scope (v1) | External content is the asset path; v1 cross-provider is text/message-only, so there's no cross-provider asset traffic to exercise this seam against. The AEAD primitive itself is available in the engine. (see DIV-3) |
| F7 | 7.1.1 | GFM-MIMI nohtml MANDATORY; no Autolink | client | ✅ Supported | Raw HTML tags are rejected fail-closed, recursively through nested parts. Haven validates and normalizes GFM-MIMI content but doesn't render it, so the no-Autolink rule holds by construction rather than by an explicit check. |
| F8 | 9.3 | No non-original-sender edit/delete without authz; clear edited/retracted indication | client | ✅ Supported | An edit or delete from anyone but the original sender is rejected unless an explicit policy authorizes it; replacements are classified as edit vs. retraction so the client can label them accordingly. |
| F9 | 4.5 | AES-128-GCM for external-content encryption | client | ⬜ Out of scope (v1) | Same basis as F6: external-content encryption is the asset path, out of scope for v1's text-only cross-provider messaging. The AEAD primitive is available; the cross-provider seam is what defers. (see DIV-3) |

### Room policy (room-policy-04)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| P1 | 3 | One role at a time; role idx 0=non-participant, 1=banned (reserved) | hub | ✅ Supported | Role indices are validated as unique with the two reserved roles enforced by name; a member can hold exactly one role at a time by construction. |
| P2 | 3/8.6 | Role-definition changes not in the same commit as participant-list changes | hub | ✅ Supported | A commit carrying both a room-policy change and a participant-list change in the same commit is rejected outright, per §8.6. |
| P3 | 5 | Fixed-membership/parent-dependent/max_clients constraints | hub | ✅ Supported | Fixed-membership, parent-dependent, and max-client/user constraints are validated and enforced before a policy is stored. |
| P4 | 8.1.x | Add/remove/kick/ban authorization via authorized_role_changes | hub | ✅ Supported | A role transition is authorized against the acting member's own role capabilities, not the target's, per §8.1.x. A regression test specifically proves the actor's own list is what's consulted. |
| P5 | 8.3 | Hub can enforce canSendMessage; other message caps client-enforced only | both | ✅ Supported | The hub enforces exactly canSendMessage, matching §8.3; every other capability is client-enforced by the spec's own design. Verified live end-to-end (a member sends, a banned member is rejected). |
| P6 | 9 | Policy authorization authoritative across all verification points | all | ✅ Supported | One stored RoomPolicy is the single authority at every hub verification point (message sends and role changes both consult it). Haven permanently disallows external joins (INV-MLS-001a), so the spec's external-join verification point doesn't apply. |

### Wire framing - protocol-06 §5 TLS presentation language (DISPATCH-119 W2)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| W1 | 5.4 | submitMessage over the real TLS-PL wire framing, not JSON | hub | ✅ Supported | submitMessage runs over the real TLS-PL wire framing, not JSON, gated by the same sender-authorization check as the JSON lane. Proven against a real MLS message posted to a running mTLS hub, then cross-checked byte-identical via the JSON lane. |
| W2 | 5.7 | requestConsent/updateConsent over the real TLS-PL wire framing | hub | ✅ Supported | requestConsent/updateConsent run over the real TLS-PL wire framing. A wire-lane grant is proven to reach the identical consent gate a JSON-lane grant would, not a separate shadow path. |
| W3 | 5.2 | keyMaterial over the real TLS-PL wire framing | hub | ✅ Supported | keyMaterial runs over the real TLS-PL wire framing and is consent-gated using the same gate the JSON lane enforces, so a wire caller is genuinely subject to it. Negotiation/signature fields decode for wire fidelity but aren't yet enforced; that would be new behavior beyond framing. |
| W4 | 5.3 | update (HandshakeBundle) TLS-PL codec | hub | ✅ Supported | The update endpoint decodes a real MLS Commit independent of the MLS library's own type, and applies a participant-list or room-policy change carried inside it; a commit mixing the custom proposal with a standard MLS proposal is refused rather than partially decoded. A conformant client needs the plaintext wire-format policy, or its handshake traffic is unreadable by a spec-conformant hub, since the MLS library defaults to encrypted framing. |
| W5 | 5.5 | notify inbound-receive over the real TLS-PL wire framing | hub | ✅ Supported | notify's inbound-receive shape runs over the real TLS-PL wire framing, verified against the draft's literal byte layout with an independently hand-computed test vector. The outbound side, this hub proactively pushing fanout to foreign followers, is a separate feature and isn't built. |
| W6 | 5.8 | identifierQuery over the real TLS-PL wire framing, DIV-4 preserved | hub | ✅ Supported | identifierQuery runs over the real TLS-PL wire framing while preserving DIV-4: the route returns a bare status with no body on both found and not-found, so response shape itself never leaks whether a request was well-formed or evaluable. (see DIV-4) |
| W7 | 5 | Directory advertises the wire lane with real path templates | hub | ✅ Supported | The directory advertises the wire lane's real path templates alongside the existing JSON-compat lane, in both the spec's nested and flat top-level shapes. A test asserts the claimed routes always match what's actually mounted, so it can't silently claim a route that doesn't exist. |
| W8 | 2 | TLS-PL is a mandatory wire encoding, not a placeholder notation (open spec question, not a Haven divergence) | hub | ✅ Supported | protocol-06 §2 literally describes TLS Presentation Language as placeholder notation for the examples, not necessarily a mandated wire encoding. This hub implements literal TLS-PL bytes anyway, as the only concrete encoding the draft actually shows; flagging the ambiguity for the working group rather than resolving it unilaterally. |

### Virtual clients (mls-virtual-clients-01, §5-§6 - DISPATCH-114 A1)

| ID | § | Requirement | Actor | Status | Notes |
|----|---|-------------|-------|--------|-------|
| VC1 | 5.2 | Derive the five per-epoch virtual-client secrets (epoch_id/epoch_base_secret/epoch_encryption_key/generation_id_secret/reuse_guard_secret) from emulator_epoch_secret | client | ✅ Supported | All five per-epoch virtual-client secrets derive correctly; proven against a real MLS library, two independent members derive byte-identical secrets from the same epoch. v1 uses the plain RFC 9420 exporter as a stand-in for the spec's Safe Exporter, which changes nothing about the derived values. |
| VC2 | 5.6.1 | Small-Space PRP: FF1 (NIST SP 800-38G), AES-128, radix 2, 32-bit numeral string, empty tweak | client | ✅ Supported | The Small-Space PRP is FF1 (AES-128, radix 2) exactly as specified, with the spec's literal bit mapping verified against the underlying library's own conventions rather than assumed. |
| VC3 | 5.6.2 | reuse_guard computation is collision-free between emulator clients and lets a recipient recover the sender's emulation-group leaf index | client | ✅ Supported | reuse_guard is collision-free between emulator clients and lets a recipient recover the sender's true leaf index; proven against a real MLS library. |
| VC4 | 5.6.3 | generation_id lets a strongly-consistent DS detect ratchet-generation collisions | client | 🟦 Implemented (not yet cross-impl-verified) | generation_id derivation is implemented and unit-tested, context-bound to group/epoch/generation/ratchet type; not yet wired into a delivery service's fan-out path in practice. |
| VC5 | 6.2/6.3 | A new emulator client can join the emulation group externally; removing one advances the epoch and all remaining members re-derive fresh secrets | client | ✅ Supported | A new emulator client can join externally and share epoch state; removing one advances the epoch and all remaining members' secrets change, proven against a real MLS library. This external-commit join is scoped to the emulation-group trust domain, distinct from Haven's product groups, which stay add-driven only. |

55 normative requirements tracked. Status breakdown:

- 🟦 Implemented (not yet cross-impl-verified): 1
- 🟦 Implemented (decision logic; durable layer pending): 1
- ⬜ Out of scope (v1): 5
- 🔴 Diverged (by design): 1
